title: Russia Bans Gasoline Exports April 1 Amid Refinery Strike Campaign; Mediazona Confirms 206,202 Russian KIA by Name
detail: Russian Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak announced a temporary suspension of all Russian gasoline exports effective April 1, 2026, citing domestic price increases caused in part by Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian refineries, fuel storage, and Baltic export terminals throughout March 2026. The ban responds to supply constraints following strikes on the Slavneft-YANOS refinery in Yaroslavl (March 28), the Ilsky refinery in Krasnodar Krai (March 27), the Promsintez explosives plant in Chapayevsk, and the sustained campaign against Ust-Luga and Primorsk Baltic ports. Simultaneously, the Mediazona/BBC Russian service reached a milestone: 206,202 confirmed Russian military deaths documented by name through independent obituary tracking. Ukraine's MoD reported cumulative Russian personnel losses reached approximately 1,297,190 as of March 30, with 1,360 losses recorded the preceding day alone.

title: Rubio Confirms US Communicated Russian Ceasefire Conditions to Zelensky; Diplomatic Gap Remains Wide
detail: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed that Washington has communicated Moscow's ceasefire conditions to President Zelensky, but that 'the final decision rests with him.' Russia continues to reject committing to a ceasefire as a precondition for holding Ukrainian elections — a Ukrainian red line — while also demanding full recognition of four annexed oblasts. France, Germany, and UK leaders have expressed concern that US mediation increasingly reflects Russian rather than Ukrainian interests. The Coalition of the Willing (35-nation grouping led by France and UK) maintained that any European troop deployment to Ukraine would only occur post-ceasefire. Zelensky's bilateral defense cooperation agreements with Gulf states, including the UAE, continue to expand Ukraine's diplomatic options as potential mediators outside the US-Russia bilateral channel.

title: Russia's Spring Offensive Intensifies Along Sloviansk-Kramatorsk Axis; Kupyansk Salient Under Pressure
detail: Russia's spring-summer 2026 offensive continues to develop on multiple axes as of March 30. Following the capture of Kovsharovka on March 29, Russian 'West' Group is pressing toward Kupyansk-Uzlovoi, threatening Ukrainian supply lines north of Sloviansk. The 'South' Group is advancing on the Sloviansk axis via Nikiforovka, preparing an assault on the Slavyansk–Kramatorsk–Druzhkovka–Konstantinovka urban agglomeration — Ukraine's fortified 'Fortress Belt.' In Zaporizhzhia, Russian pressure near Huliaipole continues with heavy bombardment. Russian forces control approximately 20% of Ukraine (~45,783 sq mi including pre-2022 Crimea and Donbas holdings). ISW assesses the Fortress Belt is unlikely to fall in 2026 given high attrition costs, but Russia appears willing to absorb heavy losses to advance frontlines before any ceasefire could freeze positions.
